headline: Early-stage collection at 2.3% of target; momentum concentrated in two regions.
collection_status: 31 responses collected against 1,350 target represents 2.3% completion. Data heavily skewed toward MARA (42%) and KIGOMA (35%); six regions have zero responses. Current pace suggests significant acceleration required to meet targets across all 10 regions.
key_findings: Expansion dominates stakeholder priorities: 48% cite expand_kf as top need, with policy advocacy (29%) second—signals strong appetite for scale but potential implementation readiness gap

Volunteer representation disproportionately high at 29% of responses; teachers and education officials underrepresented (16% combined across all teacher/official categories), risking skewed feedback on classroom realities
Scale readiness concern: avg_scale_readiness at 2.8/10 contradicts high expansion demand (4.6/10 partner satisfaction)—suggests stakeholders want growth but doubt operational capacity
Feedback themes consistently emphasize teacher incentives, government policy integration, and digital monitoring—actionable priorities across three implementation pillars
flags: Geographic blind spot: SONGWE (4 responses), MTWARA (3 responses), and six unreported regions represent 70%+ of target sample yet contribute <20% of data—high risk of unrepresentative findings
Teacher voice gap: only 9 responses from lower/upper KF teachers (29% of sample) versus 9 volunteers—insufficient data on implementation fidelity and classroom impact
Scale readiness disconnect: 2.8/10 score suggests fundamental concerns about operationalizing the expansion priorities stakeholders are demanding; root cause not yet explored
recommendation: Immediately redeploy collection effort to SONGWE, MTWARA and six silent regions with targeted teacher/head teacher recruitment. Conduct rapid deep-dive interviews (10–15) with lowest readiness respondents to diagnose scale barriers before proceeding with expansion strategy.
